input.problemTitle: Irregular electricity alerts for tube-well irrigation
input.problemDescription: Small farmers do not know when electricity is available for tube-well irrigation motors, so they waste time repeatedly visiting fields and may miss irrigation windows.

reusableSignals: Simple public dashboard for pilot metrics
repoContext.repoSummary: No repository was provided. Ghost Engineer generated recommended open-source components from the problem context.

impactBlueprint.mission: Help small and marginal farmers receive reliable electricity availability alerts for tube-well irrigation without requiring smartphones or constant internet access.
impactBlueprint.problemSummary: Farmers in villages with irregular electricity supply repeatedly visit fields to check whether motors can run. This wastes time, increases missed irrigation windows, and creates avoidable stress during crop cycles.
targetUsers: Small and marginal farmers using electric tube-well motors
impactBlueprint.openSourceOpportunity: A low-cost, documented, community-replicable alert kit can help villages adapt the solution to local electricity patterns, languages, and hardware availability.
mvpFeatures: Detect electricity availability at the pump-side control point
Send SMS alerts to registered farmers when electricity is available
Log alert delivery and manual confirmation for pilot review
Provide setup documentation for local electricians
Show a tiny dashboard for pilot metrics and field notes
technicalApproach: Use a microcontroller or GSM module to detect safe power availability signals
Send SMS alerts through a low-cost GSM module before adding any internet dependency
Keep remote motor control out of MVP until electrical isolation is validated
Document installation, failure modes, and field-testing steps in the GitHub Pack
missingParts: Farmer registration sheet or simple admin flow
recommendedOpenSourceComponents: Static Next.js or Vite pilot dashboard
Open-source documentation templates for field testing
safetyAndTrustConcerns: Do not automate motor switching in MVP
Use electrical isolation and qualified local installation
Protect farmer phone numbers and consent records

agents.summary: Structure the project as a small hardware alert kit plus clear field documentation. Keep sensor, SMS, dashboard, and pilot docs as separate modules so contributors can work independently.
topFindings: The MVP should be an alert system, not a remote-control system.
Contributor onboarding depends on wiring diagrams, setup docs, and sample alert flows.
The GitHub Pack must include field-testing and impact reporting files from day one.

risks.title: Unsafe hardware scope
risks.explanation: Adding motor control before electrical isolation is proven could create field safety risks.

risks.title: Undocumented installation
risks.explanation: If the wiring and setup path are unclear, villages cannot safely replicate the kit.

recommendations: Create separate folders for firmware, dashboard, docs, and field-testing.
Start with SMS alert proof-of-concept and a manual pilot log.
Add diagrams and safety boundaries before inviting hardware contributors.

agents.summary: A 7-day MVP is realistic if the scope stays limited to detection, SMS alerting, and manual pilot measurement. Internet dashboards and automation should remain secondary.
topFindings: SMS-first design matches low-connectivity villages better than app-first design.
A single-village pilot with five farmers is feasible.
Hardware reliability and SIM connectivity are the main build risks.

risks.title: Connectivity variance
risks.explanation: GSM delivery can vary by village and may require retry or missed-alert handling.

risks.title: Overbuilt dashboard
risks.explanation: A complex web dashboard could distract from the core field alert behavior.

recommendations: Pilot with one installation and five phone numbers first.
Measure alert success rate before adding richer features.
Use a manual fallback log when GSM messages fail.

agents.summary: The project helps farmers only if alerts are reliable, consent-based, and clearly marked as advisory. Electrical safety and phone-number privacy must be treated as core requirements.
topFindings: Remote motor control should be explicitly out of scope for MVP.
Farmer phone numbers require consent and careful storage.
False positive and missed alerts need simple field instructions.

risks.title: Electrical safety
risks.explanation: Pump-side installation can be dangerous without qualified local support and isolation.

risks.title: False trust in alerts
risks.explanation: Farmers may rely on alerts for irrigation timing, so failures must be communicated clearly.

recommendations: Add a safety checklist to FIELD_TESTING.md.
Use consent language before collecting phone numbers.
Include failure-mode instructions in the pilot guide.

agents.summary: The impact story is strong because the pain is concrete, frequent, and measurable. The MVP should prove reduced unnecessary field visits before adding broader farm-management features.
topFindings: Target user is clear: small farmers with electric tube-well motors.
Success can be measured in field visits saved and alert reliability.
The pilot should stay village-scale and locally supported.

risks.title: Adoption friction
risks.explanation: Farmers may ignore alerts if the first few messages are wrong or delayed.

risks.title: Feature creep
risks.explanation: Adding crop advice, payments, or motor control could dilute the field-testable value.

recommendations: Pitch the product as electricity availability alerts, not full irrigation automation.
Measure unnecessary field visits reduced over one week.
Work with one community partner before scaling.

comments.position: The repository should be organized for contributors before feature expansion.
comments.reasoning: Clear firmware, docs, and pilot folders make the project easier for students and field partners to improve.

comments.position: The 7-day build should prove SMS alerts only.
comments.reasoning: Anything beyond detection and alert delivery increases risk without improving the first pilot.

comments.position: Remote motor control must be deferred.
comments.reasoning: Electrical safety, consent, and alert reliability are more important than automation in the first field test.

comments.position: Pilot success should be measured by farmer behavior, not dashboard polish.
comments.reasoning: The product wins if farmers save visits and trust the alerts.

debate.finalDecision: Build the smallest SMS-based electricity alert kit, document safety boundaries, test with five farmers in one village, and use the GitHub Pack to recruit contributors for firmware, docs, field testing, and a lightweight dashboard.

contributorBoard.purpose: Build the electricity detection and SMS alert firmware.
tasks: Send SMS alert on availability state change
Add retry and manual test mode

goodFirstIssues.title: Create sample SMS alert template
goodFirstIssues.description: Add English and local-language sample messages for electricity availability and missed-alert warnings.
goodFirstIssues.label: good first issue

contributorBoard.purpose: Make the project safe and reproducible for community contributors.

goodFirstIssues.title: Draft FIELD_TESTING.md checklist
goodFirstIssues.description: Write a step-by-step field checklist for one-village pilot testing.
goodFirstIssues.label: documentation

contributorBoard.purpose: Validate alert usefulness with farmers during the pilot.
tasks: Track alert success and missed alerts

goodFirstIssues.title: Create farmer feedback form
goodFirstIssues.description: Design a simple feedback form for alert timing, trust, and visits saved.
goodFirstIssues.label: field-testing

contributorBoard.purpose: Build a tiny pilot dashboard for logs and metrics.

goodFirstIssues.title: Build static pilot metrics card
goodFirstIssues.description: Create UI cards for alert success rate, visits saved, and active pilot users.
goodFirstIssues.label: frontend

githubPack.purpose: Explain the problem, project mission, MVP, hardware assumptions, and pilot goal.
githubPack.suggestedContent:
# OpenIrrigate Alert

An open-source SMS alert kit for farmers with irregular tube-well electricity supply.

githubPack.purpose: Tell developers, field testers, and documentation contributors how to help safely.
githubPack.suggestedContent: Start with good-first-issues. Hardware changes must respect the MVP safety boundary.

githubPack.purpose: Clarify open-source reuse terms for community adaptation.
githubPack.suggestedContent: Use a permissive license such as MIT for software and document hardware notes separately.

githubPack.purpose: Show MVP, pilot, and post-pilot milestones.
githubPack.suggestedContent: MVP: SMS alert only. Pilot: five farmers. Later: dashboard and localization.
Create issues for SMS templates, safety checklist, firmware logging, and pilot metrics UI.

githubPack.purpose: Define the field pilot, safety checklist, consent flow, and failure handling.
githubPack.suggestedContent: Do not connect to live motor control during MVP. Validate alert delivery manually.

githubPack.purpose: Track impact metrics and farmer feedback after the pilot.
githubPack.suggestedContent: Measure field visits saved, alert success rate, cost per setup, and farmer satisfaction.

pilotPlan.pilotUsers: Five small farmers using electric tube-well motors.
pilotPlan.locationContext: One rural village with irregular electricity supply and basic GSM coverage.
successMetrics: Installation cost kept low enough for community replication
testingSteps: Validate safe electrical sensing with a local electrician
Register five consenting farmer phone numbers
Run alert delivery tests across different supply windows
Record false alerts, missed alerts, and farmer actions
safetyChecklist: No remote motor control in MVP
Manual override and failure instructions documented
postPilotNextSteps: Invite more contributors after safety validation
